claim: Model-driven agents self-orchestrate by looping between model and tools, eliminating the need for rigid hardcoded state machines and error-handling logic
actionable: Let agents decide their own execution path given flexible tools; provide informative error messages so agents self-correct

claim: Strands SDK provides four building blocks: model, tools, system prompt, and context—enabling agents in 8 lines of Python code
actionable: Use Strands SDK to instantiate agents with a model provider, tool decorator, system prompt, and invoke with context

claim: Decorate Python functions with @tool decorator to expose them to Strands agents without manual specification
actionable: Define Python functions with docstrings, apply @tool decorator, and Strands automatically makes them available to agents

claim: Use smaller models for classification, knowledge retrieval, and summarization to reduce cost and latency compared to always using large models
actionable: Route classification and summarization tasks to smaller models; reserve large models for reasoning and decision-making

claim: Wrap verbose tool calls (like web search) as sub-agents to summarize outputs and prevent context bloat in parent agents
actionable: Create specialized sub-agent for tool calls (e.g., web researcher with summarization prompt), wrap as tool for parent agent

claim: Swarms eliminate hierarchy: multiple specialist agents hand off control to one another based on task completion, managing context per domain
actionable: Define subject-matter-expert agents (web researcher, file editor, shell agent), use Swarm class to enable handoffs

claim: Meta-agents allow parent agents to dynamically create sub-agents for open-ended problems by passing building blocks (model, tools, prompt)
actionable: Use use_agent tool to let parent agent delegate and spawn sub-agents for sub-tasks without pre-defining them

claim: Agent conversation history is stored in memory as JSON blobs and can be accessed/edited directly via agent.messages
actionable: Access and modify agent.messages to replay or adjust conversation history

claim: Docstrings on tool functions give LLMs deeper understanding of how to use tools—critical for agent reasoning
actionable: Write detailed docstrings for all tool functions; Strands reads them to provide context to the model

claim: Workflow-based agents become rigid and unmaintainable—adding new success cases requires refactoring code; model-driven approach scales better
actionable: Avoid hardcoded orchestration logic; use model-driven agents that self-adapt to new use cases
